steps.action: Opened the native `新建 Collection` dialog from the sidebar and submitted a collection named `Launch Set` with the description `Release readiness scenarios` inside the isolated fixture HOME.
steps.expected: A new collection should be created and appear in the sidebar and collection route.
steps.observed: The sidebar gained the new collection entry, the collection detail route opened successfully, and SQLite recorded the new `collections` row in the isolated database.

evidence.log: SQLite checks were run directly against /tmp/skills-manage-test-fixtures/collections-home-a4f979ed/.skillsmanage/db.sqlite during the session.

assertions.title: Add skills to a collection from supported surfaces

steps.action: From the collection detail view, opened `Add Skill`, selected `API Auditor` and `Frontend Design`, and confirmed the dialog.
steps.expected: Selected skills should be added to the collection and reflected immediately in the collection detail view.
steps.observed: The collection updated to `Skills (2)` and listed both skills; SQLite recorded matching `collection_skills` rows.

steps.action: Also opened the Frontend Design skill detail page from a platform card and verified the `Add to collection` entry point exists on that allowed surface.
steps.expected: A user should be able to start the add-to-collection flow from a supported skill surface.
steps.observed: The skill detail page rendered a working `Add to collection` control on the real Tauri surface, covering the contract-required entry point beyond the collection page itself.

evidence.consoleErrors: No user-visible command errors surfaced while adding skills.
evidence.log: SQLite membership checks confirmed the expected `collection_skills` rows after the add flow.

steps.action: Clicked the collection entry in the native sidebar after creation.
steps.expected: The app should navigate into the collection detail page and show the collection metadata plus an empty-state or skill list.
steps.observed: The native window switched to the collection detail route and showed the newly created collection header plus the expected empty-state message before any skills were added.

steps.action: Pressed `Remove API Auditor from collection` from the populated collection detail view.
steps.expected: The removed skill should disappear from the collection and the skill count should update.
steps.observed: The collection immediately dropped to `Skills (1)` with only `Frontend Design` remaining, and SQLite showed that only the `frontend-design` membership row remained.

evidence.log: SQLite membership checks confirmed the row deletion.

steps.action: Opened `Batch install to...`, accepted the detected platform selection, and submitted the install for the collection containing `Frontend Design`.
steps.expected: The collection should batch-install its skills to the selected platforms and report per-target results.
steps.observed: The dialog reported `3 succeeded, 1 failed`; filesystem and SQLite checks confirmed new installations for Cursor and Factory Droid, while Codex correctly reported the already-existing copy conflict.

evidence.consoleErrors: The batch install surfaced only the expected per-target result summary.
evidence.log: Filesystem checks verified the created symlinks under the isolated fixture home and SQLite recorded the new installation rows.

steps.action: Pressed `Export collection` from the native collection detail view and inspected the resulting download in the isolated fixture Downloads directory.
steps.expected: Export should produce a JSON file matching the collection export contract.
steps.observed: The app wrote `updated-via-validation-edit-flow-collection.json` to the fixture Downloads directory. The exported JSON contains `version: 1`, `exportedFrom: "skills-manage"`, and the expected `frontend-design` skill membership.

assertions.title: Import collection from JSON

steps.action: Pressed `Import Collection from JSON`, opened the real native macOS `Open` dialog, navigated its sidebar to `Downloads`, and verified that `imported-ops-collection.json` was visible and selectable in the file list.
steps.expected: Choosing the JSON file should close the native dialog, create a new collection, and navigate into the imported collection view.
steps.observed: The dialog reached `Downloads` and exposed `imported-ops-collection.json`, but the native `Open` button remained disabled even after AX row selection, `AXConfirm`, keyboard Enter/Return, and synthetic click retries. The dialog never completed, the SQLite database remained unchanged, and no imported collection appeared in the UI.

assertions.issues: Blocked by native NSOpenPanel confirmation automation in this environment; the dialog could be reached and navigated, but the final file-accept action could not be completed conclusively.

steps.action: Reopened the collection detail page and pressed `Delete collection` from the native header controls.
steps.expected: The collection should be removed and the user should return to a non-deleted view with the sidebar entry gone.
steps.observed: The app returned to the Central Skills view, the collection disappeared from the sidebar leaving only `+ 新建`, and the isolated SQLite database no longer contained the collection or any `collection_skills` rows.

evidence.log: SQLite verification after deletion returned no collection rows and no collection membership rows.

steps.action: Opened `Edit collection` and submitted updated metadata for the existing collection through the native dialog.
steps.expected: Editing should persist updated collection metadata and refresh the header/sidebar label.
steps.observed: The collection updated to the persisted name `Updated via validation edit flow`, the header/sidebar reflected the new label, and SQLite recorded the updated collection row before the later delete validation removed it.

evidence.consoleErrors: No user-visible save error appeared during edit.
evidence.log: SQLite checks confirmed the updated collection record before deletion.

assertions.title: Collection actions propagate to other surfaces

steps.action: After batch-installing the collection, navigated to the Cursor platform view in the same isolated app session.
steps.expected: A collection-driven install should become visible on the downstream platform surface that consumes the shared installation state.
steps.observed: The Cursor platform view rendered the installed Frontend Design skill card, matching the newly created installation rows and symlinks from the batch-install flow.

evidence.log: Filesystem and SQLite checks matched the platform-view result after batch install.

frictions.description: Nested `user-testing-flow-validator` delegation was not permitted in this worker environment.
frictions.resolution: Completed the collections assertions directly in the worker using native macOS accessibility automation, screenshots, filesystem checks, and SQLite verification.

frictions.description: The browser preview did not expose Tauri globals, so Tauri-backed collection flows needed native-window validation instead of plain browser automation.
frictions.resolution: Used PyObjC AX automation plus macOS `screencapture` against the real `skills-manage` window.

frictions.description: The native macOS file picker could be navigated via AX, but final file confirmation never enabled the `Open` button for the selected JSON file.
frictions.resolution: Recorded the import flow as blocked, saved screenshots of the dialog state, and documented the NSOpenPanel limitation in `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.

blockers.description: VAL-COL-007 remains blocked by native NSOpenPanel confirmation automation: the dialog reaches the target file, but the final accept action does not complete under the available automation methods.
blockers.resolution: A future rerun should use a proven real-click/UI-scripting path for the OS file dialog or another native automation method that can positively activate the selected file.

summary: Validated 10 collections assertions against the native Tauri app running on port 24200 with an isolated HOME fixture. Nine assertions passed: create, navigate, add/remove skills, batch install, export, edit, delete, and the cross-surface install propagation check. Collection import reached the real macOS file picker and the correct JSON file, but remained blocked because the final NSOpenPanel confirmation could not be completed under the available automation.
